frameworks.name: linear dynamical systems
frameworks.one_line: Linear input-driven dynamics fit to PFC population responses within each context.

frameworks.name: context-dependent recurrent dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Recurrent network whose effective dynamics reconfigure with context, using transient input amplification.

frameworks.name: input-modulation / attentional gating
frameworks.one_line: Subtle contextual modulation of upstream sensory inputs drives flexible selection without recurrent reconfiguration.

core_question: What dynamical mechanisms in prefrontal cortex support context-dependent selection and integration of sensory inputs?
key_assumptions: PFC computations can be approximated within each context by linear input-driven dynamics
Population responses reflect the underlying circuit mechanism well enough to discriminate among models
Context is a discrete latent variable that reconfigures either the recurrent operator or the input gain
manipulations: Task context (which sensory feature is behaviorally relevant)
Model class (linear vs. unconstrained nonlinear dynamics)
Mechanism of contextual flexibility (recurrent reconfiguration vs. input modulation)
key_findings: Linear input-driven dynamics fit PFC responses within each context as well as unconstrained models
Two mechanisms — context-dependent recurrence with transient input amplification, and contextual modulation of inputs — fit the data equally well
Both mechanisms reveal input and recurrent-dynamics properties missing from simpler descriptions
interpretations: Population dynamics alone cannot disambiguate recurrent-gating from input-gating accounts of cognitive flexibility
Fitting constrained dynamical models places quantitative constraints on attentional modulation required in sensory areas to explain PFC and behavior
limitations_noted: Two equally consistent mechanisms remain unresolved by PFC population activity alone
connecting_signals: selection and integration of sensory inputs
